name: Cinnamon Twists
recipeIngredient: 2 Sticks Unsalted Butter, cut into bits
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor, combine the flour and butter. Pulse for 2-3 times until the mixture resembles very coarse cornmeal.
2. Add the sour cream and pulse briefly until the dough just begins to come together. Do not over mix.
3. Form into a disk, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for 4 hours or overnight.
4. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit (200 degrees Celsius).
5. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil or parchment paper.
6. In a medium bowl, combine the almonds, sugar, and cinnamon.
7. Sprinkle the almond-sugar mixture over a work surface, place the disk of dough on top, and roll out into a 9-by-12-inch rectangle approximately 1/8-inch thick, encrusting the underside of the dough with the almond-sugar mixture.
8. Trim the edges and cut into even strips, 9 inches long by 1/2-inch wide.
9. Gently pick up the ends of the strips and twist 2 or 3 times to form a corkscrew shape and place on the baking sheet.
10. Bake for approximately 15 minutes or until they are brown and crisp. Remove from the oven to a rack and allow to cool. The twists should be stored in an airtight container even for a few hours and are best eaten the same day they are baked.
Yield: Approximately 24 twists
